eval: rerank-skip gate calibration on the golden retrieval harness (live Qdrant + Cohere)
method: same 121-item golden set, measured subset = 16 qualitative-with-entity items, abstain set = 24. Three configs compared on the real index.
finding: Recall (hit@k, entity_recall) is invariant to reranking on this set. The clean-margin skip heuristic (in config B) halved abstain_recall (0.208 -> 0.083): on an out-of-catalog / false-premise query the top hybrid hit is often spuriously dominant, and only the reranker down-scores it, so skipping rerank there suppresses a correct abstain. Dropping clean-margin (config B2) restores baseline exactly.
decision: SHIP the rerank-skip gate with own-order-lookup and pool<=top_k conditions only (measured identical to always-rerank on this set: no recall or abstain regression). REJECT the clean-margin heuristic on measured evidence. Gate stays behind RERANK_SKIP (default off) as opt-in; the skip reason is logged to the trace.
safety_note: abstain_recall is a hallucination-adjacent safety metric, so a skip condition that lowers it is rejected regardless of its cost saving.
